title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Simon St-Amand
meeting_title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Leonid Shvorob and Simon St-Amand

transcript.text: Good How's it going?
Good.
Thank you.
Thank Thank you.
Yeah, so yeah, perfect.
So, where are you calling from?
Canada?
Montreal, yeah, Montreal in Canada.
Never been there, but your sounds like French a little bit.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, fully French.
Yeah, in Bali, a lot of French people come in, especially this time.
Really, yeah, leave the cold.
Yeah, okay.
So, sorry for, like, last time, it's, like, was some mess up with time zones.
No problem.
So, yeah, let's just start right away.
What's make you, first of all, jumping on this call?
Like, what's, like, changing in your processes that you think that it's a good time to talk about for new clients?
So, open, I opened a new division in a new, uh, province.
Mm-hmm.
And, uh, we're...
Having a hard time to get new clients over there, like where I'm located, we have a good reputation, we have a good base, but over there is brand new and we're having a hard time.
So I'm like, we need something new, we need something different.
So I'm curious to see what's on the market.
So new division, you mean in your region in Canada?
Yeah, so there's a different province.
I'm in Quebec, which is more French, and now we're developing in Ontario, which is more English.
So that's where we're having a problem is my team and Derek cannot help us here and we can't help us there a lot.
So that's the biggest problem.
Okay, so you're already hiring recruiters, but they're just sitting and waiting.
So they're doing some cold calls, some emails and things, but it's taking longer than I thought it would.
So they have a couple of contracts.
They have a couple of
But not that much.
I would like them to up more.
So more is like how many new contracts you want for this division, like for this quarter or for next quarter?
I would like between six to ten per month.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
So now you have like referrals, but only in Oregon and for like new division is only cold calling.
Yeah, it's only really cold call, cold email, cold outreach.
So that's always longer.
It's less motivating.
So you have some outbound system that's giving you results or it's just recruiters who are doing like...
No, only recruiters doing their thing.
So just searching LinkedIn and like DMing or what's usually...
Yeah, both.
So they search LinkedIn, they search database, they search job ads, and things like that.
And then they try to talk to people over there.
But I feel like they're having a hard time having discussion.
Once we have the discussion, it normally goes not easy, but easier.
And they're just having a hard time getting that first conversation, first meeting.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, I think you have a good reputation, like, in the market long time.
So it's not a problem with, like...
So you're targeting SPG companies, or what's your ideal customers that you want to reach?
So between 20 employees and 400 is usually where we have the most success.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
So it's like mid-level management, or what kind of roles?
Yeah, mid-level management to higher management.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
And...
What's...
It's like usually fee, like 30, 40K.
Around, I think our average right now is 16.
In US or in Canadian?
In Canadian.
Okay.
So if you make 6, 10 new placements for months, it will be a good start.
So our, our, our, our percentage of placement is usually around 40%, about 40 to 50, so if we get 3 to 6, it's at least 3 placements per month.
Yeah.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
What do you mean?
So it's like, if you have 10 conversations, you're closing 5 placements from this, or what the matter?
No, no, in 10 mandates, we close 5 normally, if they're not exclusive.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
So, you mean, if, uh,
I put you in front of 10 like hiring managers, decision makers in this mid-level SPG companies.
So usually how much of them your team could close?
Meetings depend.
If there's one conversation, I think at least 50%, if not 80%.
Usually when we get in front of the client, we normally get the job.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
So usually when like you have a new client, new company, they need only one person or sometimes two, three or more.
You mean placement?
Yeah.
Yeah.
they start with one, but normally it's around two to four per year.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
That's cool.
And it's like it's retained or contingency?
Yeah.
do contingency mostly, yeah.
Mm-hmm.
And like the cycle, how much...
How many days or weeks do need to make a placement?
Five weeks is our average.
Okay, not bad.
So it's pretty fast.
Okay, cool.
So what I also want to ask, so now your team, they're doing cold calls, so how much results do they have for one month?
How many people they can close or something?
I'm sorry, can you repeat the question?
Yeah.
So now your team is doing cold outreach?
Yeah.
So for one month, they're doing this outreach.
How many results do they have?
They're getting about two mandates per month.
Mm-hmm.
Two mandates.
Yeah.
And it's like, how big is the team in this division?
Like two, three people or more?
Two people, yeah.
Two people.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
So it looks like...
Like now, they're all day sitting and just cold calling, right?
Yeah.
Okay, gotcha.
So, I think I ask enough questions to see if that is, I think, a good fit, actually.
So, do you have any questions for me or I can explain to you how usually you work?
Could explain a little bit how you guys work and how you succeed?
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
Yeah, so, as you're saying, now that, like, generic outreach in 2026 just doesn't work because so many competitors who are using, like, different systems, some more advanced, some less.
But now just, like, cold calling, outreach, it's not so good because sometimes you can, like, see the job posts and call and they're already working with other agencies.
and so on.
So when already a role is published, it's more difficult to close, usually.
And it's just, I think, not the best efficient using the time of recruiters.
I guess they can do a good job in making a placement, but usually it's good when this process is outsourced.
And like, my experience with clients, we're working.
So what we're doing, it's really flipping the thing.
So we're not, like, waiting when, like, referrals coming or, like, cold calling, like, calling, like, five hours a day, they will succeed.
We're tracking the signals along the old market.
So, for example, in your case, we see when they're, like, SPG brands, like, mid-markets.

They're growing, so they open a new location, like you open a division, they open a new location, or they're launching a new product, or they're hiring a new leader, or they're firing some leader, C-suite.

And usually the signals means that they are growing, or they need help with hiring, so we're not reaching out just randomly, just like spraying prey, when I think it's not good for reputation of like your company, if you will just blast all the market, so we're not doing like this.

Yeah, and when we monitoring the signals, we do it daily, and we have the infrastructure, the system, they're doing this, so it's not relying like on hours of manual work of your team, and when we see this signal, usually it's like a combination, so for example, if company listing several jobs, and exact time they like.
Like some leadership changing or they mentioned they're linked in something.
So we stake in two or three signals.

And when we see it, it's their point for us to reach out for them and confirming if they're really hiring right now, if they have money and if they're really serious to make a placement of the exact role.
So in this time, introduce you like our trusted recruiting partner.
So it's not like when you're just outreaching by yourself.
Usually when it's like warm introduction, it's a good response because they needing a help exactly in that moment.
And then we introduce you as a solution.
So usually from this, like if we just start working, so first three months, let's say it's like from
So you will have access to all new intros, like we usually do in Slack channel when it sees that the new company detected.
So when we reach out to them, you see it.
And once a week, once in two weeks, I send some data.
So we have this kind of signals, then it's clear what's going on.
But it needs to be like, you know, good communications, because when we get a response from company who saying yes, it's good if like one of your people just reach them very fast.
Okay.
And how does the pricing work?
Yeah, so about pricing, it's 3k per month.
For the pilots, we do it in 90 days, usually, because just if one month, it's not enough time to get consistently.
So maybe first month, we usually have like three, like five intros.
And when we set up the system, you start seeing like positive replies and introductions from their second week.
So for 90 days, it's 10 to 15.
And that's how it's worked.
After, we can discuss in like doing more volume or depends on what kind of results we get.
So kind of like that.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah, I'm curious.
Can you send me a proposal?
I can look at it with the team and give you some news next week.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, you know, usually proposals...
They just get buried.
If you have some people who are doing business developments on your site, we can schedule a call and I will explain on the call all how we're working, get you through live their proposals.
If you have any questions, we can discuss this right away.
Okay.
Yeah.
Let me talk to them, see, and schedule something with you then.
Okay.
So, ideally, when you want to start, if, like, everything is good, like, this month?
The start of March would be good.
Okay.
So, you want, in March, already start having introductions, right?
Yeah.
So, as I said, usually it takes, like, two weeks to set everything.
So, it's good because it's, like, mid-February if we're starting, like, this week, I think first week.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate SystemHustle's lead-gen service for Proforce Personnel's new division.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Problem:** Proforce's new Ontario division is failing to meet its 6–10 mandate/month goal, securing only \~2/month via inefficient cold outreach.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)
  - [**Solution:** SystemHustle proposes a signal-based system that monitors market events (e.g., new locations, leadership changes) to generate warm, pre-qualified introductions.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=993.0)
  - [**Offer:** A 90-day pilot for $3k/month, targeting 10–15 introductions. The 90-day term is critical to demonstrate consistent results, which typically ramp up after the first month.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1312.0)
  - [**Decision:** Simon is interested but needs to review with his team. He will schedule a follow-up call, as Leonid prefers a live proposal review over a static document.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1369.0)

## Topics

### Proforce's Ontario Division: Performance Gap

  - [**Goal:** 6–10 new mandates per month.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=663.0)
  - [**Current Performance:** \~2 mandates per month.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=914.0)
  - [**Method:** Cold outreach (calls, emails, LinkedIn DMs) by a 2-person team.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=704.0)
  - [**Challenge:** Difficulty securing initial meetings; once in a meeting, the close rate is high (50–80%).](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=730.0)
  - [**Client Profile:**](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [**Size:** 20–400 employees.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [**Roles:** Mid-level to higher management.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [**Avg. Fee:** $16k CAD (contingency).](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
      - [**Avg. Placement Time:** 5 weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=897.0)
      - [**Placement Rate:** 40–50% of mandates.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)

### SystemHustle's Signal-Based Lead Generation

  - [**Strategy:** Replaces cold outreach with warm introductions.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1049.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1049.0)
    1.  [**Monitor Signals:** Track market events (e.g., new locations, product launches, leadership changes) indicating hiring intent.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)
    2.  [**Combine Signals:** Validate intent by cross-referencing multiple signals (e.g., new job posts + leadership change).](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1117.0)
    3.  [**Pre-Qualify:** Engage the company to confirm hiring need and budget.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1147.0)
    4.  [**Introduce:** Make a warm introduction to Proforce as a trusted partner.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1164.0)
  - [**Communication:** Proforce gets real-time updates in a shared Slack channel and must follow up quickly.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1260.0)

### Pilot Program & Pricing

  - [**Term:** 90-day pilot at $3k/month.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1312.0)
  - [**Rationale for 90 days:** A single month is insufficient to establish consistent results; performance typically ramps up after the first month.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1312.0)
  - [**Target:** 10–15 introductions over the 90-day period.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1312.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Simon:** Discuss the pilot program with the Proforce team.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1369.0)
  - [**Simon:** Schedule a follow-up call with Leonid and the team for a live proposal review.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1369.0)
  - [**Leonid:** Await the scheduling of the follow-up call.](https://fathom.video/share/SMGELiygjLtN5isssxkuHm1zGw75KC-U?tab=summary&timestamp=1369.0)

action_items.description: Align w/ BD team; schedule proposal review w/ Leonid; attend review; decide on 90-day pilot
